context_note: Ghana vs Panama. FTR (vig-adj): Ghana 40.5% / Draw 30.2% / Panama 29.3%. Draw price 30.2% is the HIGHEST we have ever seen - even higher than SAU-URU (24.8%) and JPN-NED (27.9%). This is essentially a three-way split: RULE8 fires at maximum strength, meaning ALL comparison 'more than' props need extra shrinkage toward 50%. RULE13 (near-identical averages -> stay near 50%) also applies strongly here. Corner handicap market (Ghana -0.5 / Panama +0.5) is essentially 50/50 (48.6% vs 51.7%), confirming the market sees these teams as very evenly matched for territorial dominance. Key data issues: Panama only n=3 usable (2 games vs South Africa, 1 vs Brazil; Dominican Republic and Bosnia games had empty stats); Panama's SOT and offsides data both have massive outliers (9 SOT vs Brazil, 5 offsides vs South Africa) that dominate the averages and require RULE12 sensitivity checks.

ghana_minus0.5_corners.note: essentially 50/50 - market sees no meaningful corner advantage

ghana_n4.note: SOT is highly opposition-dependent: 1,1 vs Austria/Germany (strong), 3,5 vs Mexico/Wales (moderate). Panama likely closer to Wales-level opposition for Ghana.

panama_n3.note: 2 additional games (Dominican Republic, Bosnia) had empty/zero boxscore statistics and were excluded

outlier_warnings.sot: 9 SOT vs Brazil is a massive outlier; without it avg=3.0
outlier_warnings.offsides: 5 offsides vs South Africa is a massive outlier; without it avg=0.5
outlier_warnings.fouls: 5 fouls vs Brazil is low outlier (RULE10 blowout pattern); without it avg=14.5

q1_ghana_more_2h_sot_than_panama.method: 2H-scaled Skellam. Ghana 2H lambda=1.25 (avg SOT 2.5/2). Panama 2H lambda: full sample (incl Brazil outlier)=2.5 gives P(Ghana>Panama)=0.26; de-outlied (avg 3.0, lambda=1.5) gives P=0.44. RULE12 sensitivity: 18pp swing -> LOW CONFIDENCE. Corner-handicap market is 50/50 (no dominant team in territory), supporting uncertainty. Ghana is modest favorite (40.5%) but the market sees no meaningful attacking dominance. RULE8 max strength (draw 30.2%) -> extra shrinkage toward 50%. Settled between de-outlied (0.44) and raw (0.26), acknowledging corner market's 50/50 signal.

q2_ghana_more_fouls_than_panama.method: Skellam: Ghana avg=9.5 (n=4, 6-13 range), Panama avg=11.33 (n=3, 5-16 range). Raw P(Ghana>Panama fouls)=0.344. RULE12: removing Panama's 5-foul outlier (vs Brazil blowout) pushes Panama avg to 14.5, making P even LOWER (~0.14) - outlier inflates Panama, signal gets STRONGER when removed. Signal is robustly 'Panama fouls more.' RULE8: draw price 30.2% (MAX trigger) -> shrink from 0.34 toward 50%, land at 0.42. RULE13: averages 9.5 vs 11.33 not near-identical but not extreme either.

q3_ghana_win.method: Direct from Smarkets FTR market, Ghana vig-adjusted 40.50%. Undeviated.

q4_2h_2plus_total_goals.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Second half over/under 1.5' market, OVER contract vig-adjusted 36.3%. Undeviated. Cross-check: Poisson from 2H team goal lambdas (Ghana 0.713 + Panama 0.567 = 1.28 total) gives P(X>=2) = 1-e^-1.28*(1+1.28) = 0.366 - consistent.

q5_ghana_3plus_sot.method: Full-match Poisson: Ghana avg SOT=2.5 (n=4), lambda=2.5. P(X>=3)=1-e^-2.5*(1+2.5+3.125)=1-0.082*6.625=0.457. RULE12: removing Wales outlier (5 SOT) -> lambda=1.67, P=0.236; removing Austria/Germany low games -> P goes higher. 20pp+ sensitivity. Settled near full-sample Poisson given Panama is a similar-level opponent to Wales (Ghana's best SOT game). Lightly shaded for draw-price conservatism.

q6_semenyo_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' market, Antoine Semenyo contract: offer-only (illiquid), mid=71.43%, x0.945 ratio = 67.5%. Full match (no 2H qualifier). Semenyo is Ghana's winger/forward, high SOT probability.

q7_fajardo_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' market, Jose Fajardo contract: offer-only (illiquid), mid=60.61%, x0.945 ratio = 57.3%. Full match. Fajardo is Panama's attacker/forward.

q8_panama_more_cards_than_ghana.method: Skellam: Ghana avg YC=2.0 (n=4), Panama avg YC=2.33 (n=3). Near-identical averages (diff=0.33). RULE13 (near-identical) + RULE8 (draw 30.2% max) both strongly indicate 50/50. Raw Skellam P(Panama>Ghana cards)=0.563, but with draw price this high and averages this close, shrink to 50%.

q9_panama_scores_first_2h_goal.method: Poisson race model using 2H team goal lambdas from Smarkets markets. λ_Ghana_2H=0.713 (from 2H Ghana O/U 0.5 market), λ_Panama_2H=0.567. P(Panama scores first 2H goal) = λ_PAN/(λ_GHA+λ_PAN) × P(at least one 2H goal) = 0.567/1.28 × (1-e^{-1.28}) = 0.443 × 0.722 = 0.320.

q10_panama_2plus_offsides.method: Poisson: Panama offsides n=3 (5,0,1), avg=2.0, lambda=2.0. P(X>=2)=1-e^-2*(1+2)=0.594. RULE12: removing outlier (5-offside vs S.Africa) -> lambda=0.5, P=0.090. MASSIVE sensitivity (50pp swing) -> LOW CONFIDENCE. This is the 8th data point in the '2+ offsides' family; all 7 prior resolved NO (RULE7 unshaded wins net +X/7). But this is the first time raw P>0.50 SIGNIFICANTLY (0.594 vs BEL-EGY's borderline 0.54). Outlier is entirely driving the >0.50 estimate. Shade DOWN heavily toward the de-outlied estimate and family pattern (NO side), landing well below 0.50.

match_facts.note: Ghana won 1-0 but with only 2 SOT while Panama had 4 SOT and 62% possession — statistical paradox. Ghana efficient.

Q1_Ghana_more_fouls.notes: Ghana did NOT have more fouls. Both on YES side but crowd further wrong.

Q2_Panama_2plus_offsides.notes: Panama <2 offsides. 2+ offsides family confirmed again.

Q3_Panama_scores_first_2H_goal.notes: Panama did not score first 2H goal (nor at all). Slight edge.

Q4_Panama_more_cards.notes: Panama more cards: YES. Our 50% vs crowd 46% — both near middle, we slightly less wrong.

Q5_Ghana_more_2H_SOT.notes: Ghana did NOT have more 2H SOT (won 1-0 with only 2 total SOT vs Panama's 4). Crowd 54% badly wrong.

Q6_Ghana_wins.notes: Ghana won 1-0. Our 40% vs crowd 48% — both on YES side, we were further from 1.

Q7_2H_2plus_goals.notes: 2H had <2 goals (Ghana only scored in 1H or neither scored in 2H). Slight edge.

Q8_Ghana_3plus_SOT.notes: Ghana only 2 SOT despite winning 1-0. Crowd 66% heavily overestimated Ghana's attacking volume (winner narrative inflation). Our 45% much more accurate.

Q9_Semenyo_1plus_SOT.notes: Semenyo had 1+ SOT. Our 68% vs crowd 56% — we were more right.

Q10_Fajardo_1plus_SOT.notes: Fajardo 0 SOT. RULE15 should have applied (Panama expected loser). We submitted 57% — should have been ~0.25-0.30. Crowd 39% was more correct.

post_match_results.learning_notes: Our best match at the time. Q8 Ghana 3+ SOT (+25.69) confirmed winner-narrative crowd inflation: crowd priced 66% for Ghana attacking output but Ghana only got 2 SOT despite winning. Q10 Fajardo (-13.80) confirmed RULE15 should apply to expected-loser forwards. Statistical paradox: Ghana won 1-0 with 2 SOT while Panama had 4 SOT and 62% possession.
